hero.eyebrow: An app for residential buildings and owners' associations
hero.titleEm: No more paper in the stairwell.
hero.lead: Announcements reach neighbors as phone notifications, rent gets paid by QR code, maintenance issues come with a photo and a status, and votes finally reach quorum. For the committee, residents, and developers alike.

showcase.rentNote: QR code to the building account

tours.sectionTitle: Four evenings we give back to the committee every month
tours.sectionSub: Today the committee does this in the evenings, with a noticeboard, a spreadsheet, and patience. Each block below replaces one manual process — and shows exactly what it looks like in the app.

t1.today: "I'll tape it up in the stairwell, and half the people will still say they never knew."
t1.p: Write it once and everyone gets a phone notification. No more paper in the stairwell, no more emails lost in spam.
t1.tick1: See how many units have read an announcement
t1.tick2: Target an entrance, a floor, or the whole building
t1.mockFoot: sent once, delivered to everyone · no notices to reprint

t2.today: "I'm back to checking bank statements and texting people about unpaid service charges."
t2.h: Rent and service charges without spreadsheets
t2.p: Charges go out with one click, a neighbor pays by QR code to the building's own account, you just confirm it. You see who hasn't paid on a single screen.
t2.tick1: Money goes straight to the building's account, never through us
t2.mockNote: one click, no texting, no awkward reminders

t3.today: "We call a meeting and not even half the ownership share shows up."
t3.h: A meeting that actually reaches quorum
t3.p: Voting by ownership share, proxies, and minutes generated straight from the results. Written resolutions without knocking on every door.
t3.tick1: The app calculates shares and quorum for you
t3.tick2: Minutes ready to download right after the vote
t3.mockDocSub: generated from the results, proxies included

t4.today: "I reported it a month ago and I still don't know if anything's happening."
t4.h: Maintenance issues with a photo, not a note
t4.p: A neighbor takes a photo, you assign a contractor, they see the progress. Every issue has an owner and a deadline.
t4.tick1: Full history at the unit and building level, nothing gets lost
t4.tick2: Whoever reported it gets notified of progress
t4.mockPhoto: photo of the issue from a resident
t4.mockFoot: Whoever reported it sees every step — nobody calls the committee on a Sunday night.

sideNotes.docsTitle: Also included: bylaws and minutes you can actually find
sideNotes.docsBody: Building documents in one place, visibility set by role. No more digging through a ten-year-old inbox.
sideNotes.disputesTitle: Also included: neighbor disputes without the conflict
sideNotes.disputesBody: A complaint is logged against a unit, not a name. The committee follows up with one click, nobody argues in the hallway.

moat.title: Others just track things. We also send the hands that fix them.
moat.body: Other building apps stop at record-keeping. Tasker Living runs on the Tasker platform, so a resident can book a vetted professional in a few clicks: cleaning, a small repair, window washing. No searching, no phone calls, with ratings.
moat.tick2: Book straight from the app, dispatch confirms the time
moat.tick3: A building with services on tap is more attractive to tenants
moat.statLine1: clients already use the Tasker platform.
moat.statLine2: We're bringing that network of vetted professionals and thousands of completed jobs into Tasker Living.

roles.title: One building, three roles, each gets its own view

vybor.h: We'll give you back your evenings
vybor.t3: Issues with an owner and a deadline

rezident.h: The whole building in your pocket
rezident.t1: Notifications instead of a stairwell noticeboard
rezident.t2: Rent paid by QR code in under a minute
rezident.t3: Tasker services a few taps away

developer.h: Hand over a building, not a binder
developer.t1: New builds delivered with the app already set up
developer.t2: Rent overview across your whole portfolio
developer.t3: Terms and rollout tailored to you

launch.title: You send a spreadsheet, we do the rest
launch.sub: No installation, no data migration, no half-day training session. Setting up the building is our job, not yours.

s1.p: Unit numbers and rents are enough. A photo of a spreadsheet works fine — we'll handle the rest.

s2.h: We set up the whole building
s2.p: We configure units, payments, and documents, and hand you access codes for your neighbors.

s3.h: You hand out codes and the building is live
s3.p: Neighbors join with a code tied to their unit. You can send the first announcement the same day.

launch.quote: "We didn't build Tasker Living from a whiteboard. We build and run our own 40-unit residence, and these exact things drove us crazy: paperwork, statements, meetings that never reached quorum. So we built the tool we wanted for ourselves, and now we're bringing it to other buildings. I launch every building personally."
launch.sig: , founder of Tasker Living · developer of Rezidence Vista Park · founder of the Tasker platform

proof.title: We're not promising. We're showing you a building where it's running.
proof.sub: No made-up client logos. One real building, 40 units, and numbers from the first weeks of actual use — we'll show them to you directly in the app on request.
proof.p2t: of payments on time, without a single text message
proof.p3t: to reach quorum on a vote — a first in this building's history
proof.p4t: paper notices in the stairwell since launch
proof.q1: "Billing and reminders used to eat two evenings a month. Now it's twenty minutes over coffee — and for the first time I'm not texting anyone about unpaid bills."
proof.q2: "I'm 71 and I don't use apps. I entered the code from the committee once, and since then the building just shows up on my phone. I knew about the water shut-off before my daughter did."
proof.q3: "I photographed a leaking valve on a Sunday, and it was replaced by Thursday. The whole time I could see on my phone what was happening — I never had to make a call."
proof.note: Residents of the pilot building, names and units shared with consent. These numbers come from real use, not a pitch deck.
proof.ctaText: Want to see it live? We'll walk you through the app on Vista Park's actual data.

pricing.title: One price, everything included
pricing.sub: No modules, no add-on fees, no three-page price list. You only pay for occupied units.
pricing.desc: About 13 Kč per unit per day. Less than a building pays for stairwell cleaning, and it saves the committee evenings every month.
pricing.i4: Issues with photos and progress tracking
pricing.guarBody: the first {{months}} free, no card, no commitment. We handle setup within {{launch}}. If it's not a fit, cancel with one message and we'll export your data to you and delete it.
pricing.underPre: Developer or investor? We tailor terms for portfolios and new-build handovers —
pricing.fine: Prices exclude VAT. We're onboarding pilot-program buildings gradually so everyone gets full support.
pricing.guarTitle: We carry the risk, not you
pricing.g2: We set up the entire building within {{launch}}
pricing.g3: The data is yours: export anytime, deleted within 30 days if you leave
pricing.g4: Cancel with one message, no back-and-forth

faq.title: What committees ask before they say yes
faq.q1: What if some neighbors don't want to use an app at all?
faq.a1: Not everyone needs to join, and not right away. The app already pays off for the committee alone: charges, documents, issue tracking. Neighbors join gradually, and every notification-backed announcement is one more reason to. In the pilot building, most people joined within the first few weeks.
faq.q2: How long does setup take, and what do we need to do?
faq.a2: Send us your unit and rent list, and we'll set up the whole building and access codes within {{launch}}. Nothing to install — the app runs in a browser and as an icon on your phone's home screen.
faq.q3: How do rent and service-charge payments work?
faq.a3: The committee issues monthly charges with one click. A neighbor pays by QR code straight to the building's own account — the money never passes through us. You confirm a payment with one click, and a reminder is one click too. Automatic bank reconciliation is coming.
faq.q4: Does this cover the legal requirement for an owners' association website?
faq.a4: The committee gets a secured space for announcements, documents, minutes, and voting — a way to make information available to owners as required by the bylaws. We're happy to go through your specific bylaw requirements with you.
faq.q5: Is the building's data safe, and what happens to it if we stop?
faq.a5: Data is stored in the European Union and protected by role-based access, so everyone sees only what they should. The committee can download a complete export (payments, votes, read receipts, documents) at any time from Building Settings. If you stop using the service, we delete all data from the database and storage within 30 days. We never hold your data hostage.
faq.q6: Do we have to switch property managers or accountants because of this?
faq.a6: No. Tasker Living adds communication, payments, and voting on top of what you handle manually today. Your accounting and technical maintenance stay exactly as they are.
faq.notePre: The pricing question is answered in the
